errors.title: Something went wrong
errors.body: We hit an unexpected error rendering this page. The error has been logged and you can try again.
errors.monitorBody: We failed to render this monitor. Try reloading, or head back to the dashboard.
errors.notFoundBody: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or was moved.

home.noMonitors: You haven't added any monitors yet.

onboarding.title: Add an alert channel
onboarding.body: Without a verified channel, alerts have nowhere to go. Email is automatic — Telegram unlocks on Pro.

channels.title: Alert channels
channels.subtitle: Where Pingup sends you alerts when a monitor goes down or recovers. Verified channels are auto-attached to every new monitor.
channels.deleteConfirm: Remove {target}? Alerts will stop going to this address.
channels.deleteConfirmTelegram: Remove this Telegram channel? Alerts will stop going there.
channels.autoAttachNote: New verified channels are automatically attached to all your monitors. Remove them here to stop delivery.

email.title: Email
email.body: Delivered via Resend. Email addresses that match your GitHub-verified email are trusted automatically.
email.addHint: If this address matches your GitHub email, it's verified instantly. Otherwise it stays pending (verification flow lands in the next release).

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.body: Instant pushes from a bot. Add a channel, then hit /verify in the bot to link it to this account.
telegram.pendingHint: Send the /verify command below to the bot to link this channel.
telegram.verifyBody: Open {bot} and send the command below. Your verification code is {code}.
telegram.lockedBody: Get instant push notifications on your phone. Pro plan unlocks Telegram alongside email.

new.nameHint: Shown in the dashboard and alerts.

heartbeat.title: New heartbeat monitor
heartbeat.subtitle: Point your cron at a unique URL. We'll alert you if the expected ping doesn't arrive.
heartbeat.intervalHint: How often your cron should ping us. Grace period is auto-set to 20% of the interval.

ping.title: New ping monitor
ping.subtitle: We'll call your URL from our side on a schedule and alert when it doesn't respond.
ping.urlHint: Must be reachable from the public internet. HTTP or HTTPS.
ping.intervalHint: How often we call your URL. Shorter intervals are Pro-only later.
ping.statusHint: We mark the monitor down on any other code.
ping.timeoutHint: A slower response is treated as down.

errors.name: Give the monitor a name.
errors.interval: Pick one of the supported intervals.
errors.url: URL must be a valid http(s) address.
errors.status: Pick one of the supported status codes.
errors.timeout: Timeout must be between 1 and 30 seconds.

detail.urlHint: Your cron hits this URL every run. Keep it secret — anyone with the link can mark this monitor up.
detail.targetUrlHint: The URL we check on each tick.
detail.exampleCurlHint: -m 10 caps wait at 10s so a slow Pingup response never blocks your cron.
detail.noChecks: No checks recorded yet. Run your cron once or hit the URL from your browser to see it here.
detail.deleteHint: Removes this monitor and all recorded checks. This can't be undone.
detail.deleteConfirm: Delete {name}? All check history will be lost. This can't be undone.
detail.publicStatusHint: Anyone with this link sees the current status, uptime, and 60-day history. No login required.

edit.title: Edit · {name}
edit.subtitleHeartbeat: Tweak the name, interval, or temporarily disable this heartbeat.
edit.subtitlePing: Tweak the name, target URL, or schedule. Changing the URL resets the live status.
edit.enabledHint: When off, the monitor stops checking and incoming pings are ignored. Existing alerts and history are preserved.

ping.urlHint: Changing this resets the current status — we'll re-evaluate on the next tick.

publicStatus.title: Publish a status page
publicStatus.body: Anyone with the link sees current status, uptime, and 60-day history — no login. Toggle off to make it private again.
publicStatus.bodyLocked: Share a public uptime page with customers. Pro plan unlocks this for every monitor.

hero.title: Monitoring that catches silent failures.
hero.subtitle: Pingup watches your sites and cron jobs. Heartbeat monitors notice when a cron quietly dies. Ping monitors hit your URLs from the outside. Alerts land in email or Telegram in seconds.
hero.ctaNote: No credit card. Free tier forever. Pay in TON when you grow.

types.title: Two ways to watch
types.subtitle: Pick the right monitor for the job. Mix and match — most teams run both.

heartbeat.title: Heartbeat
heartbeat.body: Your cron POSTs to a unique URL every run. If no ping arrives within the expected window + grace, we alert you. Perfect for jobs that die silently.
heartbeat.example: e.g. backups, weekly emails, data pipelines

ping.title: Ping
ping.body: We call your URL every 1 / 5 / 15 minutes from our side and check for a 2xx response. Perfect for sites, APIs, and status endpoints.

how.title: From zero to watching in 60 seconds

steps.title: Sign in with GitHub
steps.body: One click. No form, no config. We only read your profile and email.

steps.title: Add a monitor
steps.body: Paste a URL, or grab a heartbeat link and wire it into your cron. That's the whole setup.

steps.title: Get alerts where you look
steps.body: Email is on by default. Connect Telegram for instant push. Attach any channel to any monitor.

alerts.title: Alerts where you already look
alerts.subtitle: No extra dashboards to babysit. Add channels once, attach them to any monitor.

email.title: Email
email.body: Delivered via Resend. Goes to the same inbox you already check every morning.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.body: Instant push from a bot you /start once. Great for phone notifications that actually reach you.

pro.title: Scaling up?
pro.subtitle: Free tier gets you 3 monitors and a 5-minute interval. Pro unlocks 25 monitors, 1-minute checks, Telegram, and a public status page.

items.q: What's the difference between heartbeat and ping?
items.a: Heartbeat is passive — your cron calls us when it runs, and we alert if it doesn't. Ping is active — we call your URL on a schedule and alert if it doesn't respond. Use heartbeat for jobs (backups, reports, pipelines). Use ping for things that should be reachable from the internet (sites, APIs).
Heartbeat alerts fire within a minute of the expected window closing. Ping alerts fire on the next failed check. Telegram delivery is typically under 5 seconds end-to-end.

items.q: Does Pingup cost anything on the free tier?
items.a: No. 3 monitors and a 5-minute check interval for free, forever. Upgrade to Pro when you outgrow it — pay monthly or annually in TON.
From our Hetzner server in Europe. Multi-region checks are on the roadmap for Pro.

items.q: Can I use this for private URLs?
items.a: Yes — heartbeat monitors work for anything that can make an outbound HTTP request, including services behind a firewall. For ping monitors, the URL must be reachable from the public internet.
It's native crypto, fast, and works worldwide without card-processor drama. We're indie too — TON keeps the fees low so more of what you pay reaches us.

bottomCta.title: Stop finding out hours later.
bottomCta.subtitle: Set up your first monitor in under a minute. Free tier — no card needed.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Free forever for small projects. Upgrade to Pro when you need more monitors, faster checks, or Telegram alerts.
pricing.paidInTon: Paid in TON. Renew monthly or annually.

free.tagline: Enough to watch your weekend project.

pro.tagline: Production-grade monitoring without the Datadog bill.

pricing.alreadyActive: You're on Pro until {until}. Renewals stack from that date.

reason.monitors: You're at the {limit}-monitor limit on Free. Upgrade to Pro for more.
reason.interval: Check intervals under {interval} minutes are Pro-only.
reason.telegram: Telegram alerts are Pro-only. Email alerts work on every plan.
reason.statusPage: Public status pages are a Pro feature. Upgrade to share uptime with customers.

pricing.footnote: No card processor fees, no cancellation fees. Stop paying — we'll downgrade you to Free at the end of your paid period.

status.noChecksYet: Waiting for the first check to land.
status.historyHint: Each cell is one day. Hover for the exact uptime ratio.

subscribe.title: Pay with TON
subscribe.subtitle: Pro · {period}
subscribe.commentWarning: Important: include the comment exactly as shown — that's how we match the payment to your account.
subscribe.pollingHint: This page refreshes every 10 seconds and flips to a confirmation as soon as the payment lands on-chain.
subscribe.payBroadcast: Sent to your wallet — finalizing on-chain.
subscribe.paidBody: Your subscription is active. New monitors and Telegram alerts are unlocked.
